programme.desc: The shooting of a petty officer looks like an open-and-shut case when a Russian man is found with a recently fired gun in his car. Military crime drama, with Mark Harmon, Cote de Pablo and David McCallum.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a lance corporal, whose body has been found in an empty house, and discovers he had a number of spurned lovers and a $2million inheritance. programme.desc: The driver of a Ferrari is gunned down by a motorcyclist and the victim is identified as petty officer Colin Boxer, who was holding down a second job as a valet parker and had borrowed the sports car. During the course of the investigation, Gibbs chances on a medal awarded to Leroy Jethro Moore, the man he was named after and an estranged friend of his father. Guest starring Billy Dee Williams (The Empire Strikes Back) and Ralph Waite (The Waltons).(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. First lieutenant Michael Torres gets involved in a fight before falling to the ground, and is found beaten to death the next morning. The only witness is his friend and colleague Captain Joe Westcott, who suffers from post-traumatic stress disorder and has trouble distinguishing reality from his past military experiences in the Middle East.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tony reviews satellite footage of rebel forces in the Middle East and spots Kersey among a group of insurgents, concluding that Joe Westcott's fear of him was well-founded. Back at HQ, Abby creates a 3D computer model of the attack that occurred in Afghanistan and Joe dons a virtual-reality headset to relive the traumatic events. Will it jog his memory?(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. Lydia's Navy captain father David Wade was shot dead during the attack and her mother is overseas, so Ziva offers to look after the girl, while Abby tries to jog her memory by creating facial reconstructions of the attackers based on some grainy CCTV footage. DiNozzo and McGee find links on Rosie's laptop to possible suspect John 'Diesel' Stevens.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is called to the scene when there is an attempt on the life of FBI agent TC Fornell, with the assailant identified as Navy seaman Tyler Brown. Guest starring Julian Fellowes and featuring Alexander Armstrong in an early role.(n)

programme.desc: Dennis's first task as gaffer is to pick up a valuable antique bath from Fidel Castro's country residence and bring it back to Havana as a gift to the ambassador. Despite having made other plans for their day off, the lads reluctantly agree to join him, and a bite to eat at the Cuban leader's expense helps to lift their spirits - but Barry, Oz and Neville disappear en route. Tim Healy, Kevin Whately, Timothy Spall and Jimmy Nail star.(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet

Wyman takes a leaf out of his late father's book by seducing embassy worker Tina. Meanwhile, Neville's loyalties are tested when he is ordered to spy on a Manchester United fan, and Dennis begins to feel left out. Jimmy Nail, Tim Healy and Kevin Whately star.(n)

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are hired to create a garden as a wedding present, and find a baby under a gooseberry bush. The discovery brings out Rosemary's maternal side as the pair investigate who abandoned the child. Their task is complicated by the murder of an artistic neighbour - and the two mysterious incidents appear to be connected. Starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris.(n)

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo visit a resort in Spain to create a trophy garden for a pro-am tennis tournament run by a former champion. When one of the competitors is found dead, everyone assumes it to be a gruesome accident - but there is more to the case than meets the eye. While investigating, Laura enters the mysterious world of bullfighting, and Rosemary rediscovers her love of motor scooters.(n)

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The green-fingered amateur detectives work on a garden for an open-air theatre, where Rosemary's cousin is due to perform. However, the production suffers a setback when an actor shoots himself during rehearsal - and the gun is found to have contained live bullets instead of blanks. A member of the cast gets an attack of stage fright, and Laura is obliged to take her place in the play as the duo try to find the culprit. Drama, with Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris.(n)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries

As the past merges into the present, she is visited by the ghost of a dead policeman and realises there are still dark secrets to unearth. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes and Philip Glenister.(n)

Richard Wilson and Fay Ripley guest star.(n)

programme.desc: Drama based on the last days of cult leader David Koresh, and the 51-day siege between February and April 1993 at a compound belonging to his religious sect. In the first episode, the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, and Firearms (ATF) receives a tip-off of suspicious activity involving weapons taking place among a small religious community led by David Koresh at the Mount Carmel Center, located just outside of Waco, Texas. Starring Taylor Kitsch, Michael Shannon, Melissa Benoist and John Leguizamo.(n)

programme.title: Waco
programme.desc: ATF agent Jacob Vasquez begins undercover surveillance on the Mount Carmel compound and befriends David Koresh in hope of finding illegal weapons or other activity.(n)

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Tragedy strikes a leading football club when the team bus is hit by a train and the post-mortem examination reveals it was not an accident. Among the victims is manager Martin Bendelow, whose family has more than its fair share of skeletons in the cupboard, and more secrets are revealed when another murder takes place. Stephen Beckett guest stars, with Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team re-examines the murder of a junior doctor strangled in 2010. Interviewing her relatives, who are all doctors, staff at her hospital and a surgeon who had been the young woman's tutor, officers hope to identify a lead that was missed during the original investigation. Depressed at the thought of his daughter Holly leaving home for university, Danny finds himself being cheered by his colleagues as they hire a pedibus for a ride through central London. Tamzin Outhwaite and Nicholas Lyndhurst star.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Sam and Harry investigate when a gruesome, partly mummified corpse is found after a lorry crashes into an old factory building. The forensic experts attempt to identify the remains by reconstructing the skull, while DCI Telford and DS Linden track down a woman who claims the body is that of a former boyfriend who disappeared seven years previously. It soon transpires that the deceased had unsavoury connections. Benedict Cumberbatch makes an early screen appearance, alongside stars Amanda Burton, William Gaminara and Tom Ward.(n)

Later, he returns and takes hostages in the emergency department, and Charlie puts his life on the line to try and protect those around him. Meanwhile, Dylan risks his life for Faith, which makes her question her emotions towards him, and a frustrated Will asks Fenisha to give him a second chance.(n)

After starting the next day with an outdoor group meditation session among the wild monkeys and spectacular views, Duncan and John waste no time exploring the bustling town of Rishikesh, which attracts thousands of pilgrims from all over the world.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Reporter Hilary Andersson travels from her home in Vermont to the dark heart of America's coronavirus crisis, New York City. Meeting doctors, health workers and community activists, she asks why so many people have died in a city with some of the best health care in the world. President Donald Trump insists his own handling of the pandemic saved thousands of lives despite initially hoping it would simply go away of its own accord, but the city's leaders face criticism too. Joining food queues in the Bronx, Hilary discovers how the city's poorest have suffered the most.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: In an episode that takes place entirely from Ben's perspective, he and Phil make final plans for the job. But when Ben produces a gun, Phil decides his son is off the gang. Angry, he storms off into a confrontation with Jay and Lola, where he learns the truth about the abortion.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Sue Johnston, Alice Levine and Bob Mortimer join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: Marianne returns to Carricklea for the summer and after a night out together, she and Connell start to discuss their friendship and inevitably their attraction, but when they end up in bed, Marianne makes a request of Connell that upsets him. Later, Marianne is provoked by Alan, who ends up hitting her in the face. She calls Connell, who immediately drives over to pick her up, threatens Alan and promises her that he'll never let anyone hurt her again. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, with Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: Connell receives news that he has been offered a place to study in New York, while Marianne stays with him and Lorraine for the Christmas holidays and is embraced by their extended family. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Cuckoo
programme.desc: Two years after Cuckoo went missing in the Himalayas and was presumed dead, Ken and Lorna are looking forward to a future without the kids under their feet. Dylan is about to go to university and Rachel is rebuilding her life with new boyfriend Ben, but the arrival of mysterious stranger Dale threatens to mess up life in the Thompson household. Greg Davies, Taylor Lautner and Helen Baxendale star.(n)

programme.title: Cuckoo
programme.desc: Dylan plans an elaborate party as a means to seduce Zoe. Dale alights on Cuckoo's potato van while searching for his next career move. Comedy, starring Greg Davies, Taylor Lautner, Tyger Drew-Honey and Holly Earl.(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Martin Clunes hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Fin Taylor and Janet Street-Porter joining Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Aspiring movie mogul Riandra and her cousins plan a wedding with a cinematic theme. They have already written the script and cast the soon-to-be newlyweds in the starring roles.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood investigate the spookier side of nature, including a tree that bleeds, a fungus that likes to eat ant brains and a flesh-chewing fly.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood check out some mind-boggling armies from the natural world, including some sleepy suburbs invaded by 10 million bugs.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends dig up Roman treasure at Hadrian's Wall.(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: A look back at some of Lindsey Russell and Richie Driss's most adrenaline-fuelled challenges.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores the world of parasites, including tapeworms, mosquitoes and cuckoos, investigating the skills they use to exploit their hosts.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall investigates the skills and senses that creatures around the world use to hunt and survive. This programme focuses on the speed and acceleration of the cheetah, the elastic tongue and 360-degree vision of the chameleon, and a shark with a hidden sixth sense.(n)

programme.title: Miniature Britain
programme.desc: Biologist George McGavin travels around Britain with a revolutionary new microscope camera to provide a close-up view of the natural world. The film reveals how caterpillars' feet have hooks that anchor them upside down to leaves, butterfly and moth wings are a kaleidoscope of colourful scales that deter predators, and birds' feathers have thousands of hooks that zip together to keep them airborne. The film-makers also show miniature life in cities, such as creatures grazing the moss on pavements and the legions of dust mites scavenging for food in everyday homes.(n)

programme.title: Ice Age Giants
programme.desc: Alice Roberts sets out to discover why the creatures that dominated the planet 40,000 years ago died out as the world started to warm up and the end of the Ice Age neared. She examines how the woolly rhino was unable to adapt when the increase in snowfall prevented it eating enough grass, and learns that mastodons turned on one another.(n)

programme.title: The Good Dinosaur (2015)
programme.desc: In a world where the asteroid impact that wiped out the dinosaurs never happened, humans have evolved as a primitive species while the giant lizards remain the dominant life form on Earth. A young apatosaurus becomes separated from his family, but finds a friend in a feral human boy as he makes the dangerous journey home. Pixar animated adventure, with the voices of Raymond Ochoa and Jack Bright.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for the Factory
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the living history series in which a group of women step back in time to experience factory life in the past. The year has moved on to 1973, when manufacturing output and productivity was at its highest since the war. The women are tasked with bright orange flares, a must-have fashion item of the decade, and with business booming, targets have to be met. However, when a male machinist joins the production line, his end-of-week pay packet sends shockwaves around the factory.(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Chef Brian Turner takes guests back to their youths by visiting locations important to them and then cooking dishes inspired by their childhoods. In the first edition he is joined by cookery writer and former Great British Bake Off judge Mary Berry.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at the television appearances made over the years by Days of Wine and Roses actor Jack Lemmon, capturing the highlights and milestones of his life and career. Narrated by actress Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Some Like It Hot (1959)
programme.desc: After witnessing a Mob hit in Chicago, two male jazz musicians don drag and hide out with an all-girl band to evade the gangsters pursuing them. However, the pair struggle to disguise their attraction for the beautiful lead singer - while one of them catches the eye of a smitten millionaire. Billy Wilder's classic romantic comedy, starring Jack Lemmon, Tony Curtis and Marilyn Monroe.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at television appearances made over the years by American film actor Tony Curtis, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career, which led to him becoming considered a Hollywood star. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking them to auction.(n)

programme.title: Dam Busters: The Race to Smash the German Dams
programme.desc: James Holland presents an analysis of the 1943 air attacks by the RAF, in which 19 Lancaster bombers ventured into the industrial heartland of Germany to destroy three heavily defended dams with the new bouncing bomb. He argues that the raid was the best-executed and most important of all time and explores whether its true impact has been underestimated - and if the result should have been even greater.(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The members of Walmington-on-Sea's Home Guard platoon are excited when they hear they have been selected to appear in an army training film - but Captain Mainwaring refuses to take part when he finds out he and his men are to play Nazi soldiers. Classic comedy first aired in 1975, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier.(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the growth of Kuala Lumpur, from a swampland tin mine to a major British colony, before becoming a major economic powerhouse in its own right. He meets jewellery at the Royal Selangor pewter factory, samples Malaysia's national dish, and investigates the scandalous behaviour of an infamous British resident general at the turn of the 20th century. He then heads to Melaka, for centuries one of the greatest trading ports in the world, before finally arriving at the tip of the Malaysian peninsula in the historic city of Johor Bahru.(n)

programme.title: Ella Fitzgerald: Just One of Those Things
programme.desc: The life and times of the jazz singer, beginning with a 1934 talent contest at the Apollo Theatre in Harlem, and following her next five decades, reflecting the passions and troubles of the times in which she lived in her life and her music. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo, with contributions from Laura Mvula, Ray Brown Jr, Tony Bennett, Johnny Mathis, Jamie Cullum, Cleo Laine and Smokey Robinson.(n)

programme.title: Jazz Divas Gold
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances by acclaimed female jazz artists from shows including Not So Much a Programme - More a Way of Life, Morecambe and Wise, Parkinson and Show of the Week. Featuring Ella Fitzgerald, Peggy Lee, Blossom Dearie, Nina Simone, Cleo Laine, Marion Montgomery, Betty Carter, Sarah Vaughan, Eartha Kitt and Amy Winehouse.(n)

programme.title: Julieta (2016)
programme.desc: A chance encounter with a childhood friend of her estranged daughter causes a guilt-ridden Madrid woman to rethink her future. Pedro Almodovar's drama, starring Emma Suarez and Adriana Ugarte. In Spanish.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode's alphabetical journey through the BBC's culinary archive continues as he reaches the letter L. The Hairy Bikers cook a lasagne and Jamie Oliver bakes a lemon and lime cream tart.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher heads Hampton Court, not far from his west London home, to see how the team there keep the gardens and grounds in prime condition, taking the reins of shire horses as they pull and harrow the ground for the summer ahead. Kate Humble reports from her Wye Valley home patch on how local food producers are rallying round during the crisis, and Adam Henson turns out a new Gloucester calf onto spring pasture.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don reveals how to pot tomatoes on to the next stage of their growth. He also pots up tender vegetables and plants containers for summer colour. Adam Frost enjoys the seasonal plants in his garden and shares plans to transform a part of his plot into a seating area using a few logs and plants. Arit Anderson fills a range of imaginative containers with a variety of plants, while Frances Tophill explores spring flowering trees and their benefits for bees. Plus, how one man has designed his home around his love of houseplants.(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits his favourite local loch, while Euan Mcilwraith explores all the hues of yellow in the spring countryside. Arlene Stuart finds out about the team of conservationists who moved in together so they could carry on their work. Plus, the traditional flour mill that's ground to a halt.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George comes up with a novel way of using old pots to create an easy rockery, while Brian builds a mini mountain feature with just stone and sand.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Middle East
programme.desc: Turkey is one of only a handful of nations that has the ability to be self-sufficient. Its hugely diverse landscape produces a spectacular range and quantity of produce that defines its local cuisine. In Istanbul, Nigel gets a taste of the exuberant dishes of the Sultans, before travelling to Central Anatolia where he meets families who are working hard to preserve their traditional food culture in the face of mounting modernity. The food writer also explores the Eastern Black Sea region, where he discovers the truly mosaic-like quality of Turkish culture and cuisine.(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives, ideal to draw upon when planning the perfect picnic. The menu includes Paul Hollywood's pork pies with quail eggs, a superb sandwich from James Martin and Ainsley's own mustard, bacon and onion tarts.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the seven remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room for reduce, reuse and recycle week. Joe Lycett kicks the group off with a pattern for a man's bomber jacket, which must be pieced together from four second-hand women's garments. In the transformation challenge, the sewers are tasked with changing laundry bags into a stylish and wearable garment, before finally being tasked to use old knitwear to make a made to measure jumper dress.(n)

programme.title: Oklahoma! (1955)
programme.desc: A farm girl is torn between two very different suitors - a charming cowboy wandering through the territory and a brooding farmhand with a dark side to his nature. Meanwhile, another cattle driver has finally raised enough money to marry his sweetheart, only to find he has a rival. Rodgers and Hammerstein's musical Western, with Gordon MacRae, Shirley Jones and Rod Steiger. Songs include Oh What a Beautiful Mornin' and I Cain't Say No.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: An insight into the life and work of actor and director Richard Attenborough, with vintage interviews and classic archive clips illustrating a career that made him one of British cinema's most popular and successful figures. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Dunkirk (1958)
programme.desc: Soldiers are separated from their comrades-in-arms during the Dunkirk evacuation and face a terrifying journey to safety. A heroic lance corporal takes charge, aiming to lead the platoon to sanctuary while those around him panic. Second World War adventure, starring John Mills, Richard Attenborough, Robert Urquhart and Bernard Lee.(n)

programme.title: Burma with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon ventures into Burma's highlands, where he attends a fire balloon festival and visits an elephant sanctuary, where the animals can enjoy a peaceful life after working in the logging industry. He also makes a secret journey into one of the country's conflict zones to meet members of a rebel army fighting against the Burmese military.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a compilation of some of the most memorable inventions from the show. The programme features a prototype for pedal-powered aviation, a device to stop couples from straying into each other's side of the bed, and the youngest ever entrepreneur to be backed by the Dragon.(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an isolation version of his topical comedy show from his garage. A diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life - including Romesh's mother - are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today and the latest talking points in the media. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined remotely by a selection of celebrity guests, comedians and experts, to investigate how and why the events in the news matter to people across the country.(n)

programme.title: Dave
programme.desc: Emma's car is broken into and her laptop is stolen. Meanwhile, Elz, keen to make an impression on Trippie Redd, takes on the role of babysitter.(n)

programme.title: Dave
programme.desc: Dave meets with music company executives while pitching for a record deal. He ends up being introduced to Benny Blanco and Justin Bieber, and gets invited to his first Hollywood party, where he rubs shoulders with Kourtney Kardashian.(n)

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: Vengeance for a nasty incident has key Mayans aiding their Stockton brethren, and Emily tries bribery back in Santo Padre.(n)

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: EZ and Angel confront their mother's killer, resorting to tactics that are hard to cover up, while Galindo family members face strategic challenges.(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from viewers at home on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: A woman who did not have children until her 40s prepares for the birth of her second son, who was diagnosed with Down's Syndrome early in the early stages of her pregnancy. A couple whose previous child was stillborn at 40 weeks are now preparing for the birth of another baby, but have been tormented with anxiety that the same thing will happen again. A first-time mother fears she will not be a natural parent, a concern that is exacerbated when there is a long delay before she can hold her baby.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth, Joe Swift and the team end a week of programmes looking back at the best of the horticultural event by focusing firmly on the public's favourite gardens, plants and personalities. There is a look at how the Queen and members of the royal family have been involved over the years and Sophie and Joe reveal the designer who has won the BBC/RHS People's Choice Garden of the Decade.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Jay Blades' Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay and his guests demonstrate how to grow potatoes, replace broken guttering, build a shoe bench and create a loft hatch.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Retired couple David and Joyce's back garden in their home outside Gloucester looks more like an allotment than a garden. David is a dedicated vegetable grower and for Joyce, that means there's nowhere shady or pretty to sit and enjoy the garden. Meanwhile, David's increasing back and knee problems mean he is struggling to keep up its maintenance. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich set out to make the garden both prettier and more user friendly, all within a £3,000 budget.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Joe Swift take a look back over the last decade of world-class design and horticulture from the RHS Chelsea Flower Show from their own gardens, beginning by celebrating the very best of British. From roses to royalty, they reveal more about the people and plants that fuel the nation's obsession with gardening, and meet the designers and the passionate people inspired by the UK's rich history and landscape.(n)

programme.title: Canterbury Cathedral
programme.desc: Observational documentary series following a year in the life of this 11th-century building, which is the home of the Church of England and the worldwide Anglican Communion, as well as being a Unesco World Heritage Site. Having taken over from Rowan Williams in February 2013, the Most Rev Justin Welby celebrates his first Christmas as the Archbishop of Canterbury, while the cathedral's Great South Window is taken down for restoration, and a new girls' choir makes its mark after 1,400 years of male domination of the choral tradition.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money.(n)

programme.title: Big Dreams Small Spaces
programme.desc: In Essex, Denise has never ventured beyond the decking area behind her house but harbours ideas of having an altogether 'dream-like' garden, including building ruins of a mill to remind her of childhood. Her husband Dave isn't quite as enthusiastic, not least the amount of work involved, so Monty Don is called in to mediate. Meanwhile in Suffolk, father-and-daughter team Rob and Jo's ambitious plan to build a productive terraced allotment on a treacherously steep slope in the middle of a wooded wasteland leaves Monty lost for words.(n)

programme.title: Puttin' on the Ritz: The Genius of Fred Astaire
programme.desc: Fred Astaire's definitive performance of the song and dance routine, as recorded in the late 1920s by Irving Berlin, and later featured in his 1946 musical Blue Skies.(n)

programme.title: Pal Joey (1957)
programme.desc: A roguish singer dreams of owning his own nightclub. However, he finds himself in an enviable dilemma because of two women - one a wealthy socialite, whom he romances in the hope she will finance the enterprise, and the other a naive chorus girl, the true object of his affections. Musical drama, with Frank Sinatra, Rita Hayworth and Kim Novak. Songs include The Lady Is a Tramp, My Funny Valentine and Bewitched, Bothered and Bewildered.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Althorp in Northamptonshire, where experts Charles Hanson and Will Axon find a selection of antiques and collectibles to take to auction.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle present a selection of tales of romance and heartache at Longleat, including the burgeoning relationship between two rare Amur tigers.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Suzanne and Danny have wildly differing views on how to improve their cramped 1960s bungalow in Bedfordshire. With three children and only two usable bedrooms, they're in urgent need of help, but while Suzanne wants style and a wow factor, Danny is fixated on storage and practicality. With the couple at loggerheads, architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison go head to head to win the commission. Their task is to radically transform this dysfunctional space into a spectacular and practical home, all working to the couple's £70,000 budget. Presented by Angela Scanlon.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.sub-title: Inside the Factory: Keeping Britain Going
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace catches up with the Heinz baked bean factory in Wigan, which has upped production to deliver almost 50 million cans in just a month, nearly double the number of tins the firm would normally sell in the same period. Operations manager John Brockley reveals how staff are keeping safe across the site, including passing through a thermal camera that automatically takes the temperature of workers as they enter the factory.(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Adventures
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull explore their favourite inventions and scientific breakthroughs from the years between 1850 and 1900, beginning at Crossness Pumping Station in south-east London, which was part of the redevelopment of London's sewerage system. They then head to London Zoo, where they get their hands on a first edition of Charles Darwin's masterwork On the Origin of Species.(n)

programme.title: Comedians: Home Alone
programme.desc: Comedians perform a selection of quickfire sketches, songs and isolation anecdotes, filmed by themselves in and around their own homes during the coronavirus lockdown. In the opening instalment, Bob Mortimer's Train Guy comes to life in an animation, Michael Spicer takes on Priti Patel in his viral hit Room Next Door and married comedians Rachel Parris and Marcus Brigstocke go up against each other in a lip sync batte.(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Maisie Adam, Ed Byrne, Sophie Duker, Ed Gamble and Mark Simmons and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga traces the history of an 18th-century sea captain's house near Bristol's docks with links to the slave trade. Bristol was a major port in the transportation of slaves from Africa's Guinea Coast to the Caribbean, and both the man who built the house and its first full-time resident were heavily involved in the trade. David uncovers stories of piracy, abandoned babies, a household slave who made a daring escape from the property, and a protest on the doorstep as the abolitionist movement gained momentum.(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary filmed over the course of a year following life on five farms around Scotland. It is autumn on Lewis in the Outer Hebrides and sea shepherd Sandy gathers in a flock of wild sheep by boat, while on the mainland in the east Mel and Martin head to market to look for the perfect rams to impregnate their ewes. In central Scotland near Loch Lomond, hill farmers Bobby and Anne health-check their flock to make sure they are fit to breed, and in the far north John has 4,000 ewes to check, but is also expecting thousands of visitors from all over the world as he hosts the World Sheepdog Trials.(n)

programme.title: Masters of the Pacific Coast: The Tribes of the American Northwest
programme.desc: Part two of two. Archaeologist Dr Jago Cooper reveals how the American Northwest cultural tradition managed to survive against the odds. After European contact, indigenous people of what is now southwest Alaska, British Colombia and Washington State suffered disease, theft and oppression, and Cooper argues that Northwest Coast culture has an extraordinary resilience.(n)

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: In a prequel to Inspector Montalbano, the detective moves to a remote village in the Sicilian mountains, where he investigates the case of an attempted murder at the hands of a vulnerable woman, whose motives appear unfathomable. Italian drama series set in the early 1990s, starring Michele Riondino.(n)

programme.title: Own the Sky: Jet Pack Dreamers
programme.desc: The story of Australian David Mayman, whose passion for the tantalising possibilities of jetpacks became an obsession. Filmed over 10 years, the documentary follows his seemingly impossible quest to realise his childhood dream to build and fly the world's first jetpack - an ambition that almost cost him his life.(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll America
programme.desc: The mid-1950s saw Elvis Presley make his TV debut with Heartbreak Hotel, following it with a gyrating version of Hound Dog that outraged the conservative media. A 'cleaned-up' artist was required, and Christian family man Pat Boone sated that need. While Jerry Lee Lewis caused upset with Whole Lotta Shakin and the Everlys shocked with Wake Up Little Susie, bespectacled geek Buddy Holly calmed things down with his school friends the Crickets. Contributors include Jerry Lee Lewis, Don Everly and Tom Jones.(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll America
programme.desc: Examining the era between Buddy Holly's death in early 1959 and the Beatles landing at JFK in spring 1964, when rock 'n' roll seemingly calmed down, went uptown and got spun into teen pop. The genre fuelled the Motown sound in Detroit and sound-tracked guitar instrumental groups on the west coast, such as the Ventures and Beach Boys, as it helped birth increasingly polished pop sounds across the States. Contributors include Jerry Lee Lewis, Ben E King and Chubby Checker.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Andy Crane host the edition first shown on September 14, with performances by artists including Damian, Madonna, Technotronic featuring Felly, Tears for Fears, Sydney Youngblood, Tina Turner, Black Box and Aerosmith.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first shown on September 21, featuring performances by London Boys, Aerosmith, S'Express, Richard Marx, the Wonder Stuff, WASP, Black Box and Janet Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Masters of the Pacific Coast: The Tribes of the American Northwest
programme.desc: Part two of two. Archaeologist Dr Jago Cooper reveals how the American Northwest cultural tradition managed to survive against the odds. After European contact, indigenous people of what is now southwest Alaska, British Colombia and Washington State suffered disease, theft and oppression, and Cooper argues that Northwest Coast culture has an extraordinary resilience.(n)

programme.title: BBC Young Musician
programme.desc: Three violinists, a cellist and a harpist perform works including music by Debussy, Saint-Saëns and Kreisler in the strings final, aiming to win a place in the BBC Young Musician 2020 semi-final. Organist and conductor Anna Lapwood introduces highlights from the Royal Welsh College of Music and Drama in Cardiff, and is joined by acclaimed British violinist Matthew Trusler to review each of the performances.(n)

programme.title: Highlands: Scotland's Wild Heart
programme.desc: The course of the seasons, beginning with spring, which sees the hills on fire on fire as the annual muirburn takes place in the Cairngorms. As the flames rise, an osprey returns to a valley near Loch Ness, with its movement captured in super slow-motion at 800 frames per second. Red squirrels and roe deer prepare for the breeding season, while a cataclysmic downpour has major consequences for a family of dippers, whose youngsters are forced to fledge early before the nest is swept away. Narrated by Ewan McGregor.(n)

programme.title: The Golden Age of Canals
programme.desc: Documentary charting the changing face of Britain's canals after the Second World War, as enthusiasts fought to save the inland waterways from destruction - despite facing strong opposition from the government. Amateur and professional footage illustrates the canals' transformation from industrial thoroughfares to tourist attractions during the 1950s and 1960s, as well as efforts to restore and preserve the network's locks and boats. Narrated by Gina McKee.(n)

programme.title: Dracula by Northern Ballet
programme.desc: Bram Stoker's gothic romance is brought to life on stage in this ballet, performed by the dancers of Northern Ballet to music by Schnittke, Rachmaninov, Pärt and Daugherty. Choreographed by David Nixon OBE and recorded at Leeds Playhouse on Halloween 2019.(n)

programme.title: Ballrooms and Ballerinas: Dance at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of dance featured on the BBC, including profiles of stars such as Victor Silvester demonstrating how to do the twist or encouraging us to dance like John Travolta.(n)

programme.title: Unprecedented
programme.desc: A compendium of three short plays written and performed in isolation - Viral by James Graham, aboput three teenagers in pursuit of the next internet craze, Penny by Charlene James, starring Lennie James as a man living in a hotel wrestling with his own survival, and Going Forward by John Donnelly, in which a team leader takes drastic measures to ensure targets are met.(n)

programme.title: Unprecedented
programme.desc: Stories of love, triumph and uncertainty during the time of coronavirus, focusing on the lives of couples, NHS workers, families and teenagers whose lives have changed dramatically. This episode features Romantic Distancing, written by Tim Price and starring Ines De Clercq and Arthur Darvill, Safer at Home, written by Anna Maloney and starring Gemma Arterton, Geraldine James and Rory Keenan, and House Party, written by April De Angelis and starring Patricia Allison, Risteard Cooper, Gabriel Harland, Cecilia Noble, James Norton, Rhashan Stone, Meera Syal, Olivia Williams and Fenella Woolgar.(n)

programme.title: Unprecedented
programme.desc: Two short plays written and performed in isolation: Grounded, by Duncan Macmillan, tells the story of a woman who is worried about her elderly parents' cavalier attitude to the virus. However, when she is overcome by the stress of her work, it seems that she is the one who needs looking after. Katherine Parkinson, Alison Steadman, Michael Elwyn and Arlo Dollimore star. Fear Fatigue, by Prasanna Puwanarajah, is based on conversations with NHS workers in March 2020, and documents the feelings and fears of frontline staff in the days and weeks before the coronavirus lockdown. Starring Paterson Joseph, Rory Kinnear, Esh Alladi, Natalie Dew, Anna Madeley and Jodie McNee.(n)

programme.title: Unprecedented
programme.desc: Three new lockdown dramas. Kat and Zaccy, written by Deboran Bruce, is about a son ignoring his mother's emotional pleas to come home from university during lockdown. Monica Dolan and Alex Lawther star. The Unexpected Expert, by Matilda Ibini, tells the story of a woman who worries that the crisis will have a huge impact on her social care package. Saida Ahmed, Lucianne McEvoy and Golda Rosheuvel star. The Night After, by Josh Azouz, stars Kathryn Hunter and Marcello Magni as a couple in their sixties who try to make contact with the outside world.(n)

programme.title: BBC Young Musician
programme.desc: Three violinists, a cellist and a harpist perform works including music by Debussy, Saint-Saëns and Kreisler in the strings final, aiming to win a place in the BBC Young Musician 2020 semi-final. Organist and conductor Anna Lapwood introduces highlights from the Royal Welsh College of Music and Drama in Cardiff, and is joined by acclaimed British violinist Matthew Trusler to review each of the performances.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross demonstrates the creation of the perfect brown-toned scene of a mountain at sunset.(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Diagrams
programme.desc: Marcus du Sautoy explores the stories behind some of the most familiar scientific diagrams, beginning with Leonardo da Vinci's masterwork Vitruvian Man. The piece, drawn in the 1480s, combines the artist's passions for anatomy and geometry, and also illustrates an ancient architectural riddle set out 1,500 years earlier by the classical writer Vitruvius - a puzzle that still fascinates experts to this day.(n)

programme.title: Andrew Marr's History of Modern Britain
programme.desc: The journalist examines Britain under Margaret Thatcher, claiming her efforts to restore confidence and pride in the country led to a dramatic and divisive transformation of British society. He also examines the consequences of her emphasis on privatisation before revealing how her views continue to influence the British national identity.(n)

programme.title: Rome's Lost Empire
programme.desc: Dan Snow uses the latest satellite technology to reveal the secrets of ancient Rome. Teaming up with archaeologist Sarah Parcak, the presenter sets out to identify and then track down lost cities, amphitheatres and forts in an adventure that sees him travel through some of the most spectacular parts of the vast empire.(n)

programme.title: Rome's Invisible City
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by Dr Michael Scott to explore the underground treasures that made Rome the powerhouse of the ancient world. Using 3D scanning technology, he uncovers the lost subterranean world of the Colosseum, as well as the aqueducts and sewers that supplied and cleansed it, and helped run civilisation's first metropolis and its empire.(n)

programme.title: Seven Ages of Britain
programme.desc: David Dimbleby traces 2,000 years of history through the art and artefacts that chart the social, political and cultural development of Britain. He begins by looking at the results of the country's repeated invasions, from 43AD to 1066, with his studies taking him through Britain to Italy, Turkey and France, revealing such treasures as Roman mosaic work, Jarrow Monastery and the Bayeux Tapestry.(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Buster and Ladonna bond over a memorable trip to the movies, prompting Arthur to suspect Ladonna has taken his place as Buster's best bud.(n)

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: The Farmer tortures everyone with his piano-playing, while the Flock decide to have a party.(n)

programme.desc: Millie is now a teenager and has her first crush, while Craig gets a Saturday job, Lauren takes a hike and Dad is not best pleased when Amber's ex turns up.(n)

programme.desc: Millie enters the school spelling competition, hoping she will get more attention from Dad - but when Fran also decides to give it a go, Amber and Dad promise not to take sides.(n)

programme.title: Millie Inbetween
programme.desc: Millie is looking forward to going ice skating and getting some time with Dad, while Jake's real father is putting in a rare appearance to take him to the air Guitar Championships.(n)

programme.title: Millie Inbetween
programme.desc: Overhearing Millie describe her as 'uncool', Mum decides it is time to change. However, the results are epically embarrassing for Millie and Lauren.(n)

programme.title: Millie Inbetween
programme.desc: When Mum is made redundant from her job at Sunnyshopper, the family is supportive - until she starts a regime of frenzied over-tidying. Millie realises Mum is bored and frustrated, so suggests something positive - that she starts her own business. The family brainstorms - and the hunt is on to find a product that she can market.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson introduce a shocking collection of weird clips from the natural world, this time featuring a man who was struck by lightning, an everlasting storm and a shocking secret lurking in the swamps of South America.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.sub-title: Rollercoasters, Great Walls And Tiger Festivals
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his CBBC mates go on an adventure to find the most unusual and amazing places in Asia. They try out their chopstick skills on yummy Japanese street food; enjoy a hair-raising ride on the world's fastest rollercoaster in Abu Dhabi; make a song and dance about the Great Wall of China; explore a museum full of superheroes in Thailand and become big cats in a Tiger Dancing Festival in India! Ed is joined on this bonkers road trip by Hacker and Dodge, Lauren Layfield, Cel Spellman, Iain Stirling, Inel Tomlinson and Naomi Wilkinson - there's stuff to do in Asia that is totally ace and it turns up All Over The Place!(n)

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Two sisters plan to put a very 'on-trend' wedding for their mother and soon to be step-dad with a fashion-themed big day. With the help of Instagram sensation, make-up expert and Zayn Malik's sister, Doniya Malik, the girls put on a fashion show like you've never seen before, complete with a real catwalk fashion show.(n)

programme.title: Show Me What You're Made Of
programme.desc: Five British children travel to the other side of the world to see first-hand how their favourite things are made. They begin their trip on a cocoa farm in Ghana.(n)

programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges.(n)

programme.title: Millie Inbetween
programme.desc: Tired, stressed and living in each other's pockets, Dad and Amber realise they need some space - so decide to take a break from their relationship. The kids are gutted, but determined that their own friendships will survive. Their efforts to stay in touch bring Dad and Amber into contact - and their frosty relationship starts to thaw. Meanwhile, Mum has problems of her own when she decides slacker Craig needs a hobby.(n)

programme.title: Millie Inbetween
programme.desc: Dad and Amber are back together - and Amber's moving in. It's only temporary, until they find a bigger place, but the resulting chaos drives Millie, Lauren, Fran and Jake round to Mum's. And Millie has an ulterior motive: getting Craig to look after the hyper Jake as revenge for his annoying behaviour. She thinks it'll teach him to be a better brother - but Jake thinks Craig is pretty cool as he is, and starts imitating his teenage ways. Back in the confusion of the crowded flat, Dad's new website is bringing in lots of work, but his Post-It based filing system has a major weakness: gusts of wind.(n)

programme.desc: It's just another day at the Furchester Hotel, that is until the Furball, a bouncing furry monster ball, arrives in a flurry of bouncing and chanting! Elmo asks why they are all shouting 'Furball' and Furgus says that it's because the Furball has arrived, it's the best game ever, whenever he turns up at the hotel they all play a game of 'Furball', he's very excited and goes to get his Furball equipment bag and everything is there except his Furball shoes. Furgus and Funella go to look for his shoes and tell Phoebe and Elmo to keep an eye on the Furball and not let him out of their sight. Just at that moment, the Furball disappears, only for them to spot him up on the balcony, they're soon chasing him all over the hotel. programme.desc: John Craven is at home in his garden, where he gives tips on what butterflies to look out for and how to get involved in a nationwide survey. programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Eltham Palace in South London. Finds include a collection of Gerry and Sylvia Anderson puppets, two unknown paintings by Mexican Marxist artist Diego Rivera, whose work documented working class culture in the 1960s, a well-preserved mammoth tooth, a newspaper printing plate telling the story of the moon landings, and letters written by an Army officer captured by the IRA in the 1920s.(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Saying goodbye to Puducherry, the celebs journey north to the foothills of the Himalayas for their final week in India. Following a treacherous drive up narrow, winding roads, they arrive at their new home on the outskirts of Rishikesh, ready to experience the spiritual side of life in Northern India. After starting the next day with an outdoor group meditation session among the wild monkeys and spectacular views, Duncan and John waste no time exploring the bustling town of Rishikesh, which attracts thousands of pilgrims from all over the world.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Reporter Hilary Andersson travels from her home in Vermont to the dark heart of America's coronavirus crisis, New York City. Meeting doctors, health workers and community activists, she asks why so many people have died in a city with some of the best health care in the world. President Donald Trump insists his own handling of the pandemic saved thousands of lives despite initially hoping it would simply go away of its own accord, but the city's leaders face criticism too. Joining food queues in the Bronx, Hilary discovers how the city's poorest have suffered the most.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: In an episode that takes place entirely from Ben's perspective, he and Phil make final plans for the job. But when Ben produces a gun, Phil decides his son is off the gang. Angry, he storms off into a confrontation with Jay and Lola, where he learns the truth about the abortion.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Sue Johnston, Alice Levine and Bob Mortimer join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: Marianne returns to Carricklea for the summer and after a night out together, she and Connell start to discuss their friendship and inevitably their attraction, but when they end up in bed, Marianne makes a request of Connell that upsets him. Later, Marianne is provoked by Alan, who ends up hitting her in the face. She calls Connell, who immediately drives over to pick her up, threatens Alan and promises her that he'll never let anyone hurt her again. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, with Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: Connell receives news that he has been offered a place to study in New York, while Marianne stays with him and Lorraine for the Christmas holidays and is embraced by their extended family. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.desc: Ayesha and Bear anxiously await the response when their BAME donor campaign film goes live. Elsewhere, Deborah brings a shocked Daniel and Zara up to speed about Ruhma's houseguest, and Karen helps a woman trapped in a war between her grieving family.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Flambeau enlists Fr Brown's help to steal the Queen's coronation gift from the Pope. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to Herefordshire with a house-hunting couple who have a £600,000 budget. He also visits a potato farm run by the same family for almost 100 years.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Reporter Hilary Andersson travels from her home in Vermont to the dark heart of America's coronavirus crisis, New York City. Meeting doctors, health workers and community activists, she asks why so many people have died in a city with some of the best health care in the world. President Donald Trump insists his own handling of the pandemic saved thousands of lives despite initially hoping it would simply go away of its own accord, but the city's leaders face criticism too. Joining food queues in the Bronx, Hilary discovers how the city's poorest have suffered the most.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: In an episode that takes place entirely from Ben's perspective, he and Phil make final plans for the job. But when Ben produces a gun, Phil decides his son is off the gang. Angry, he storms off into a confrontation with Jay and Lola, where he learns the truth about the abortion.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Sue Johnston, Alice Levine and Bob Mortimer join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: Marianne returns to Carricklea for the summer and after a night out together, she and Connell start to discuss their friendship and inevitably their attraction, but when they end up in bed, Marianne makes a request of Connell that upsets him. Later, Marianne is provoked by Alan, who ends up hitting her in the face. She calls Connell, who immediately drives over to pick her up, threatens Alan and promises her that he'll never let anyone hurt her again. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, with Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: Connell receives news that he has been offered a place to study in New York, while Marianne stays with him and Lorraine for the Christmas holidays and is embraced by their extended family. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Community Life
programme.desc: Cruse Bereavement Care NI on grieving and loss and an appeal for volunteers by the MACS young people's charity.(n)

programme.title: Cuckoo
programme.desc: Two years after Cuckoo went missing in the Himalayas and was presumed dead, Ken and Lorna are looking forward to a future without the kids under their feet. Dylan is about to go to university and Rachel is rebuilding her life with new boyfriend Ben, but the arrival of mysterious stranger Dale threatens to mess up life in the Thompson household. Greg Davies, Taylor Lautner and Helen Baxendale star.(n)

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The Covid-19 pandemic has plunged the world economy into a deep recession. How long will it last and what kind of recovery can we expect? That in part depends on what governments do now. Should they be piling up future debt to cope with today's crisis? HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the former Governor of India's Reserve Bank and IMF Chief Economist Raghuram Rajan. Do the old rules of fiscal discipline no longer apply?(n)

Ainsley also visits the city of Petra, learning the history of the Bedouin people and exploring the astonishing Treasury building. programme.title: Love Your Garden

programme.title: Grow Your Own at Home with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Advice on growing fruit and vegetables, with Alan sharing the tips and tricks he uses on his own plot, with his Love Your Garden team members Katie Rushworth, Frances Tophill and David Domoney joining in from their homes. From seriously stylish ways to grow veg anywhere to practical tips on what to plant and where, they show how to get gardens bursting with fruit, salad, herbs and vegetables to supply the table for the year ahead and beyond.(n)

programme.title: Martin Clunes: My Travels and Other Animals
programme.sub-title: The Good, The Bad and the Ugly
programme.desc: Martin learns about animal exploitation - the good, the bad and the ugly. The actor visits Sri Lanka, where fishing for manta rays is legal, but a turtle sanctuary offers hope. In Thailand, he learns more about silk production - as well as a more animal-friendly alternative - and on the Australian Friday Island, he is shown the delicate art of growing pearls. Finally, in the Abrolhos islands, Martin learns about the most valuable catch in Australia's waters.(n)

programme.title: Countrywise

Guest reporter Sheila Hancock returns to the quiet corner of the Dorset coast where she first fell in love with the countryside almost 80 years ago.(n)

Ainsley also visits the city of Petra, learning the history of the Bedouin people and exploring the astonishing Treasury building. programme.title: Martin Clunes: My Travels and Other Animals
programme.sub-title: The Good, The Bad and the Ugly
programme.desc: Martin learns about animal exploitation - the good, the bad and the ugly. The actor visits Sri Lanka, where fishing for manta rays is legal, but a turtle sanctuary offers hope. In Thailand, he learns more about silk production - as well as a more animal-friendly alternative - and on the Australian Friday Island, he is shown the delicate art of growing pearls. Finally, in the Abrolhos islands, Martin learns about the most valuable catch in Australia's waters.(n)

programme.title: Countrywise

Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas, John Cleese, Julie Andrews, Jennifer Saunders and Rupert Everett.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to Chessington to create a car-themed garden for hospital worker and automobile fan Alan Rendall. In July 2014, he managed to rescue his unconscious neighbour, Roy Whiteway, from his burning house, but was unable to save Dorothy, Roy's wife, as the fire was so intense. To recognise his bravery, Alan and the team surprise the whole Rendall family and turn their featureless plot into an automobile-inspired haven.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Hemel Hempstead, Hertfordshire, where they meet deaf couple Asif Iqbal and wife Sabina. The green-fingered experts attempt to turn a boring lawn and crumbling patio into a Bollywood dream garden with an outdoor kitchen for Asif, Sabina and their family.(n)

programme.desc: Oliver's recovery hits a setback and David continues his quest for kicks when he approaches some youths on bikes and purposely shoves one of them over. programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Up the Khyber (1968)
programme.desc: British forces in colonial India find themselves in deep trouble when a manic local ruler discovers the soldiers' fearsome reputation is thoroughly undeserved - prompting open rebellion. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Roy Castle, Joan Sims, Bernard Bresslaw and Terry Scott.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a well-known business tycoon leads Morse to investigate the man's feuding family, and inadvertently makes him the unwitting target of a hate campaign by the tabloids - a problem compounded by his uneasy relationship with a new chief superintendent. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star alongside Gwen Taylor, Martin Clunes, Alun Armstrong, Anna Massey, Charlotte Coleman and Rupert Graves.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A walk in the woods leads to tragedy for Emily Simpson, who witnesses something unsettling before being found dead in her cottage by old friend Lucy Bellringer. Convinced natural causes are not to blame, Lucy calls in DCI Barnaby and Sergeant Troy, whose investigation eventually uncovers sinister long-buried secrets. The first ever episode of the long-running detective drama, guest starring Jessica Hynes and Emily Mortimer.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Farmer Alex Preston is found dead at the sacred Crowcall stone circle in Midsomer Mow. Suspicion initially falls on local druids led by high priests Ezra Canning and Leticia Clifford, since Preston's plans to plough Gorse Meadow would have barred their access to the site. Barnaby and Jones are joined by new pathologist Kate Wilding to investigate, and as the case wears on, the inspector becomes convinced greed rather than spiritualism is the motive. Starring Neil Dudgeon, Jason Hughes and Tamzin Malleson.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A local woman is found murdered following a row with her husband at a residents' association Spanish-themed evening. The finger of suspicion soon points to an odd-job man with a violent history, and the chairman of the association reveals he heard a squeaking noise after the victim left - but the witness promptly winds up dead himself. Crime drama, starring John Nettles.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Danny and Alice try to keep their new romance a secret, but have more pressing concerns when she is attacked by a lioness - one of several animals on the loose after the storm. Relations between Leopard's Den and Mara reach breaking point when Vanessa refuses to supply drugs to help dying elephant calf Tula, while Du Plessis is delighted when Caroline arrives at the party.(n)

programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Community spirit in the village of Morton Fendle is dealt a blow by the collapse of a craft centre in which many locals had invested - and Alan Hollingsworth, the businessman behind the project, has become a public hate figure. Barnaby investigates when Hollingsworth's wife disappears, and seems to have been kidnapped. However, when Hollingsworth is subsequently murdered and his wife reappears safe and sound, he suspects an elaborate scheme. Roger Allam guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits a new age commune, where a man has fallen to his death, or may have been pushed. The dead man turns out to have a connection to the leader's shady past - they set the commune up together as a confidence trick, but the leader came to believe in what he preaches. A further death raises greater suspicion, especially since a vast inheritance might be coming the group's way. Guest starring Stephen Moyer (True Blood).(n)

programme.title: Carry On Don't Lose Your Head (1966)
programme.desc: A cunning English nobleman puts his talent for disguise to good use by rescuing aristocrats from execution during the French Revolution. However, the leader of the secret police is determined to unmask the elusive hero spiriting the nobility out from under the guillotine. Comedy spoof of the Scarlet Pimpernel story, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Joan Sims, Jim Dale and Charles Hawtrey.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a woman is drowned in a bath and made to look like a bride, with a chilling lipstick message on the bathroom mirror, it triggers a spate of ghoulish wedding-themed murders in Great Worthy. The case takes Barnaby and Jones to a donkey sanctuary, a heritage steam railway and a pub run by an ex-policeman and former brothel madam as they try to identify the killer. Guest starring Pam Ferris (Rosemary & Thyme) and Kacey Ainsworth (formerly EastEnders' Little Mo).(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Julia McKenzie returns as the sleuthing spinster in the first of three murder mysteries. While staying at a hotel in the Caribbean, Miss Marple investigates the death of a fellow guest, refusing to believe he died of a heart attack. With the help of a business tycoon she sets about finding the real cause of the man's demise, and is soon drawn into a web of deceit and dark magic. Adapted by Charlie Higson and co-starring Antony Sher, Robert Webb, Montserrat Lombard, Daniel Rigby, Hermione Norris, MyAnna Buring, Warren Brown and Oliver Ford Davies.(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: Six-part drama based on the Windermere novels by Paula Daly, following the lives of three women and the personal struggles each of them face. Over-stretched, working mother Lisa never lives up to the expectations she sets herself, and cannot help but compare herself to her female friends, Kate and Roz. Behind closed doors however, each of the women struggle with the pressures to keep up the image of having the 'perfect life'. Starring Anna Friel, Rosalind Eleazar and Sinead Keenan.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective gains a taste of prison life while investigating the murder of an inmate jailed for a property swindle. The victim's two partners-in-crime are also incarcerated in the same low-security cell block. Morse is determined to find out whether they know anything about the killing, but has to avoid offending the prison authorities while also dealing with a brash subordinate. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with guest appearances by Sean Bean, Richard Wilson, Jim Broadbent, Diana Quick and Steven Mackintosh.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The detective discovers his half-sister's daughter has committed suicide and sets out to find what prompted it, entering the world of teenage rave parties and drug-taking. Meanwhile, Lewis has his sights set on promotion and studies for exams. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Sorcha Cusack, Jason Isaacs and John Junkin.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Following the loss of Leopard's Den, only Danny and Du Plessis remain in South Africa, desperate to raise enough money to buy a new reserve. When pregnant Alice arrives from the UK unannounced, she realises the pair have been lying to her about their jobs, and things go from bad to worse for Danny when a confrontation with Peeters pushes him to breaking point. Robert Bathurst (Cold Feet) joins the cast.(n)

programme.desc: A spate of petty thefts is traced to a local nursing home, but Blaketon's investigations uncover something far more sinister. Meanwhile, David's first day in his new job goes spectacularly wrong, but Bernie is not surprised. Rural drama, starring Jason Durr and Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers take in a lodger, and Mildred is delighted when he begins doing all the household jobs that George will not tackle. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)

programme.desc: Chrissy discovers she has an admirer, while Mr Roper thinks his wife is seeing someone else. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan and Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The year is 1940, and Sam is made homeless when a bomb destroys her boarding house, alerting Foyle to a system of organised looting around Hastings. However, when a corpse is found on the beach, the investigation brings the team into conflict with British attempts to secure vital American aid for the war effort. Police drama, starring Michael Kitchen, with Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ashfordly Hall throws open its doors to the public, but all hell breaks loose when a collection of valuable Constable sketches is stolen. Mike and Tom are sent to question the chief suspect, an 80-year-old art thief, only to find him mugged and bound to a chair - with none of the paintings anywhere in sight. Nostalgic drama, starring Jason Durr and Ryan Early.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy is interviewed by the police about Nick. Kim and Dave go househunting. Kim is determined to confront Tina about going away on holiday with Frank.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tina loses her passport ahead of her holiday with Frank. Nick is due to appear in court. The vicar is shocked when Zoe and Emma tell him the blessing ceremony is for them.(n)

programme.desc: Raquel is thrilled to be asked to be Betty's bridesmaid, while Bet tells staff she and Rita are going into partnership. Fiona is pleased to see Steve and Vicky looking miserable.(n)

programme.desc: Rita has second thoughts about investing in the Rovers, Maxine continues to chase Des despite him continuously standing her up, and Mavis offers to take the Kabin off Rita's hands.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders

Mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Richard Briers and Judy Parfitt.(n)

Mrs Bird's soup kitchen receives a helping hand from Daisy and Mrs Patmore. Costume drama, starring Penelope Wilton and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Oxford plays host to a veteran film star, in town to visit a local cinema. However, a mysterious death casts a sinister pall over the occasion, and offers the newly promoted DS Endeavour Morse a fresh case to pursue. Meanwhile, a family visit puts DCI Thursday's hospitality to the test. Inspector Morse prequel, starring Shaun Evans and Roger Allam.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Sam and Foyle are shot at and almost run over by a speeding vehicle, and discover the incident is part of an illicit fuel racket. An undercover investigation reveals the detective's son Andrew to be involved, and the discovery that a driver at the petrol company has been murdered complicates matters further. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden.(n)
